topic: Deniz progress report — finally holding the comb right

turns.content: good news today my friend... small good news, but for a barber it is big
I like good news! What happened?

Deniz... he finally holds the comb right. I know this sounds like nothing to you. but for months I have been telling him... the comb sits between the index and the middle finger, the thumb guides, the wrist does the work. today I looked over and he was practicing on the mannequin head... and his hand was correct. the angle, the pressure, everything

That doesn't sound like nothing at all — the comb hold is the foundation of everything else. If that's wrong, everything built on top of it will be off. Did you say anything to him, or did you let him have the moment?

I did not say anything... if you make a big thing out of it, the young ones get self-conscious and they lose it. and the beautiful thing is... I do not think he noticed he was doing it differently. when it becomes natural, you do not notice. like breathing. that is when you know it is real

That's a perfect way to describe it. Conscious effort becoming unconscious skill — that's the transition from beginner to someone who's actually learning the craft. It sounds like you're cautiously optimistic about him?

cautiously... yes. he is 17, he has a long way to go. the comb is step one. the scissors are step two. the razor... that is step 100. but today I thought... okay, maybe this one has something. Hasan usta always said 'the blade knows if the hand is lazy, and the hand knows if the heart is lazy.' I think Deniz has the heart. the hands will follow. inşallah

That's a beautiful line from your master. If Deniz has the heart and you have the patience and knowledge to guide the hands, that's a strong combination. It must feel rewarding to pass it along the way it was passed to you.

it does... when you teach someone, you keep the craft alive. the straight razor, the hot towel, the way you hold the man's head just so... these things cannot be learned from a video. they need hands on hands. anyway... I am getting sentimental. it was just the comb. but sometimes the comb is enough to make an old barber happy
Sometimes the comb is everything. Enjoy the good day, Mehmet.
